tasks.name: Initialize Next.js Project
tasks.description: Create a new Next.js project using `create-next-app` with specified configurations (TypeScript, Tailwind, App Router, ESLint, src-dir).
tasks.implementationGuide: Run: `npx create-next-app@latest eventy360 --ts --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --use-npm`. Navigate into the created directory `cd eventy360`.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Project directory `eventy360` is created with standard Next.js files and folders. `package.json` includes necessary dependencies.
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Pseudocode provides specific commands (`npx create-next-app`, `npm install`, `npx shadcn-ui init`, `git init`, etc.) and config points (`i18n.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `.env.local`). Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. Acceptance Criteria: Local dev server runs, basic Arabic RTL page renders, Shadcn usable, Supabase project exists, code pushed to GitHub.
tasks.summary: Next.js project initialized using create-next-app with TS, Tailwind, App Router, ESLint, and src directory.

tasks.name: Install Core Dependencies
tasks.description: Install essential project dependencies including Supabase client, Shadcn UI, form handling, validation, and i18n libraries.
tasks.implementationGuide: Run: `npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr next-intl shadcn-ui react-hook-form zod @hookform/resolvers`. Verify installation in `package.json` and `package-lock.json`.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Dependencies are successfully added to `package.json` and `package-lock.json`. `npm install` completes without errors.
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Pseudocode provides specific commands (`npx create-next-app`, `npm install`, `npx shadcn-ui init`, `git init`, etc.) and config points (`i18n.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `.env.local`). Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. Acceptance Criteria: Local dev server runs, basic Arabic RTL page renders, Shadcn usable, Supabase project exists, code pushed to GitHub.
tasks.summary: Successfully installed core dependencies: @supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr, next-intl, shadcn-ui, react-hook-form, zod, @hookform/resolvers using npm. Dependencies verified in package.json.

tasks.name: Configure Shadcn UI
tasks.description: Initialize and configure Shadcn UI for component management and integration with Tailwind CSS.
tasks.implementationGuide: Run: `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`. Follow interactive prompts, accepting defaults or adjusting paths as needed (e.g., for `utils.ts`, `globals.css`). Ensure `tailwind.config.js` and `components.json` are updated.
tasks.verificationCriteria: `components.json` is created/updated. `tailwind.config.js` includes Shadcn theme settings. `globals.css` includes base styles. Utility function file exists.
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Pseudocode provides specific commands (`npx create-next-app`, `npm install`, `npx shadcn-ui init`, `git init`, etc.) and config points (`i18n.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `.env.local`). Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. tasks.summary: Shadcn UI appears to be configured. components.json and src/lib/utils.ts exist with correct content. src/app/globals.css contains base styles/variables. Note: Standard tailwind.config.js/ts was not found, but configuration might exist elsewhere.

tasks.name: Setup Supabase Project & Environment
tasks.description: Create a new Supabase project via dashboard or CLI. Configure local environment variables for Supabase URL and Anon Key.
tasks.implementationGuide: Use Supabase Dashboard (Project -> New project) or CLI (`supabase login`, `supabase init`, `supabase projects create <name> --org-id <org> --region <region>`). Obtain Project URL and Anon Key. Create `.env.local` file in the project root. Add `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=<Your_URL>` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=<Your_Anon_Key>`. Ensure `.env.local` is in `.gitignore`.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Supabase project is accessible via the dashboard. `.env.local` file exists with correct keys. File is listed in `.gitignore`.
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Pseudocode provides specific commands (`npx create-next-app`, `npm install`, `npx shadcn-ui init`, `git init`, etc.) and config points (`i18n.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `.env.local`). Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. Acceptance Criteria: Local dev server runs, basic Arabic RTL page renders, Shadcn usable, Supabase project exists, code pushed to GitHub.
tasks.summary: Supabase project 'eventy360' (lbemsvyhgphdoobpbexp) confirmed to exist. User manually created .env.local with project URL and Anon Key. Confirmed .env.local is correctly ignored by .gitignore.

tasks.name: Configure i18n (next-intl) for Arabic
tasks.description: Set up `next-intl` for Arabic (`ar`) locale only, including configuration files, middleware, message file, and RTL layout adjustments.
tasks.implementationGuide: 1. Create `i18n.ts` configuring `ar` locale and message loading. 2. Create `messages/ar.json` (e.g., `{}`). 3. Create `middleware.ts` using `next-intl` middleware example. 4. Modify `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx`: Add `dir="rtl"` to `<html>`. Wrap children with `<NextIntlClientProvider messages={messages}>`. Ensure necessary imports and props (`params: {locale}`) are handled.
tasks.verificationCriteria: `i18n.ts`, `messages/ar.json`, `middleware.ts` exist and are configured. `layout.tsx` applies RTL direction and includes provider. Basic app navigation respects locale prefix (`/ar`).
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Pseudocode provides specific commands (`npx create-next-app`, `npm install`, `npx shadcn-ui init`, `git init`, etc.) and config points (`i18n.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `layout.tsx`, `.env.local`). Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. Acceptance Criteria: Local dev server runs, basic Arabic RTL page renders, Shadcn usable, Supabase project exists, code pushed to GitHub.
tasks.summary: Configuration files (i18n.ts, middleware.ts, messages/ar.json) and layout modifications (src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx for RTL and IntlProvider) were verified to be already in place. Task status updated to completed.

tasks.name: Initialize Git Repository & Push
tasks.description: Initialize a local Git repository, create an initial commit with the project setup, and push it to a new GitHub repository.
tasks.implementationGuide: 1. Create a new repository on GitHub. 2. Run `git init -b main`. 3. Ensure `.gitignore` includes `.env.local` and `supabase/.temp/`. 4. Run `git add .`. 5. Run `git commit -m "feat: Initial project setup"`. 6. Run `git remote add origin <GitHub_Repo_URL>`. 7. Run `git push -u origin main`.
tasks.verificationCriteria: Local Git repository is initialized. Initial commit exists. Code is successfully pushed to the specified GitHub repository.
tasks.analysisResult: The analysis covers initializing Next.js (App Router, TS, Tailwind, Shadcn, `next-intl` for `ar`/RTL), creating the Supabase project, installing dependencies, and setting up Git/GitHub. Assumes standard CLI execution and service availability. Steps are logical; minor config variations possible. Follows best practices (Git init, secrets handling). Potential issues: network errors, key/permission problems, `next-intl`/App Router nuances. Acceptance Criteria: Local dev server runs, basic Arabic RTL page renders, Shadcn usable, Supabase project exists, code pushed to GitHub.
